reason: Both answers correctly diagnose 0.5% as normal TOFU behavior, correctly identify BOFU content as the primary lever with an order-of-magnitude better conversion, and correctly caution that gating suits sales-led (not product-led/trial-led) motions. They're very close in quality and structure. The differentiator is the 'check which existing pages already convert' key point: Answer B explicitly frames step 1 as auditing which existing posts already drive signups and doubling down on those, directly matching that key point. Answer A's parallel step instead says to tune CTAs on the 'top 10 posts by traffic,' which conflates traffic volume with conversion performance — a subtly weaker/less precise version of the same idea. Otherwise the two answers are nearly interchangeable in correctness and actionability.
